questions.question: What is the judiciary?
questions.correct_answer: The branch of government that interprets laws and delivers justice.
questions.explanation: It resolves disputes and ensures laws are followed.
questions.source_section: What is the Role of the Judiciary?

questions.question: What is the main role of the judiciary?
questions.correct_answer: To interpret laws and protect the rights of citizens.
questions.explanation: Courts ensure justice and fairness in society.

questions.question: What is the highest court in India?
questions.explanation: It is the final court of appeal and the guardian of the Constitution.

questions.question: What are the three levels of courts in India?
questions.correct_answer: Supreme Court, High Courts and District Courts.
questions.explanation: These levels form the hierarchical structure of the Indian judiciary.

questions.question: What does independence of the judiciary mean?
questions.correct_answer: Courts can make decisions without interference from the government.
questions.explanation: This ensures fair and unbiased judgments.

questions.question: What is Public Interest Litigation (PIL)?
questions.correct_answer: A case filed in court for the benefit of the public.
questions.explanation: It allows people to seek justice for those whose rights are violated.

questions.question: What is an appeal in the judicial system?
questions.correct_answer: A request to a higher court to review a lower court’s decision.
questions.explanation: People can approach higher courts if they are not satisfied with the judgment.

questions.question: What is the difference between civil and criminal cases?
questions.correct_answer: Civil cases involve disputes between individuals, while criminal cases involve crimes against the law.
questions.explanation: Courts handle both types of cases to maintain justice.

questions.question: How does the judiciary protect Fundamental Rights?
questions.correct_answer: By allowing citizens to approach courts when their rights are violated.
questions.explanation: Courts can order actions to restore rights and stop injustice.

questions.question: Why is the judiciary important in a democracy?
questions.correct_answer: It ensures justice, equality and protection of rights.
questions.explanation: An independent judiciary prevents misuse of power.

notes.title: Judiciary
notes.content: The judiciary is responsible for interpreting laws and resolving disputes between citizens and the government.

notes.title: Structure of the Judiciary
notes.content: India has a hierarchical judicial system consisting of the Supreme Court, High Courts and District Courts.

notes.title: Independence of Judiciary
notes.content: Judges are independent and can make decisions without pressure from the executive or legislature.

notes.title: Public Interest Litigation
notes.content: PIL allows individuals or groups to approach the court to protect public interest and rights.

notes.title: Protection of Rights
notes.content: Courts ensure that citizens’ Fundamental Rights are protected under the Constitution.

facts.fact: The Supreme Court is the highest court in India.
Public Interest Litigation allows people to file cases for public welfare.
An independent judiciary is essential for a democratic country.
Courts can declare government actions unconstitutional if they violate the Constitution.
